executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 Canadian organizations publicly listed as victims in the past 48 hours across all sectors. High-profile targets include Canada Goose (hit by two groups: CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), and multiple public sector entities including Rural Municipality of Gimli, Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health, Thunder Bay Counselling, Workers Health & Safety Centre, and Eastern Townships School Board. The Qilin ransomware group alone is responsible for 19 victims, followed by Play (10), Safepay (10), and Clop (14). This represents a coordinated mass exploitation event requiring immediate organizational vigilance and incident response readiness.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a severe ransomware crisis with 103 organizations compromised in 48 hours spanning all provinces and sectors. Critical infrastructure targets include healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Thunder Bay Counselling, Peterborough Public Health, Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal, Teccart College), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), and multiple municipal governments. No CCCS advisories were identified in this feed.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was hit by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Active Green + Ross breached by Sinobi, Kids & Company breached by Sinobi.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware; Morison Insurance Brokers breached by Sinobi; multiple accounting firms compromised (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Bergmanis Preyra law/accounting).

report_2w.executive_summary: Cisco ASA and Firepower devices are under active attack via three N-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363), with CISA and the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issuing urgent warnings. Meanwhile, the npm package ecosystem has been hit by multiple supply chain attacks, including a compromised Bitwarden CLI package that stole developer credentials and SSH keys. The UK Biobank suffered a major breach exposing health data of 500,000 individuals, now listed for sale in China. North Korea's Lazarus group is actively targeting macOS users with ClickFix social engineering tactics, and the threat actor ShinyHunters claims breaches of Udemy and Anthropic's Claude Mythos AI model data.

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL25-012 on April 23, 2026, warning of active exploitation of Cisco ASA and Firepower vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363). This represents a critical threat to Canadian infrastructure as Cisco firewalls are widely deployed. No breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported during this period.
